description: Pleasingly graceful and stylish in appearance or manner; refined and tasteful. 'Phiro' names the quality of refined beauty—not merely attractive but shaped by taste and care, the polish that comes from attention.
sound_symbolism: The word carries refined poise. 'Phi' /ɸi/ begins with soft precision, breath shaped carefully. Then 'ro' holds steady presence, the elegant composure.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'phiro thiloma' (elegant garden). Related to 'luwae' (graceful) but emphasizes refinement and taste rather than natural flow.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: True elegance arises from simplicity—the refined quality that remains when all excess is removed.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Elegance combines beauty with intention—Art Nouveau sought forms that were both natural and refined.
